meta.title: Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: An end-of-day deterministic reflection tool covering three psychological axes: Locus of Control, Contribution vs Entitlement, and Radius of Concern.

nodes.text: Good evening. You made it through another day — that matters. This is a space to reflect honestly, not to judge yourself. There are no right answers here, only true ones. Let's take five minutes to look at your day together.
If today had a weather forecast, what would it be?
options: Sunny — things went the way I wanted
Cloudy — okay, but something was weighing on me
Stormy — it was genuinely hard
Unpredictable — wild swings, I couldn't read the day
answer=Sunny — things went the way I wanted:A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH
answer=Cloudy — okay, but something was weighing on me:A1_Q_AGENCY_MID
answer=Stormy — it was genuinely hard:A1_Q_AGENCY_LOW
answer=Unpredictable — wild swings, I couldn't read the day:A1_Q_AGENCY_MID

nodes.text: You said today felt like "{OPEN_Q1.answer}". When something went well, what made it happen?
options: I had prepared and it showed
I adapted quickly when something shifted
The team came through and we moved together
Honestly, I got lucky — circumstances aligned

nodes.text: You said today felt like "{OPEN_Q1.answer}". When things got complicated, where did your mind go first?
options: What can I still control here?
Who dropped the ball — was it me or someone else?
How do I get through this without it getting worse?
I wanted someone to step in and take charge

nodes.text: You said today felt stormy. When things got hard, what did that feel like from the inside?
options: Like forces outside me were driving the day
Like I had tried everything but nothing worked
Like I'd lost the thread and couldn't find it
Like I needed someone to rescue the situation
answer=I had prepared and it showed:A1_Q_CHOICE_INTERNAL
answer=I adapted quickly when something shifted:A1_Q_CHOICE_INTERNAL
answer=The team came through and we moved together:A1_Q_CHOICE_MIXED
answer=Honestly, I got lucky — circumstances aligned:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXTERNAL
answer=What can I still control here?:A1_Q_CHOICE_INTERNAL
answer=Who dropped the ball — was it me or someone else?:A1_Q_CHOICE_MIXED
answer=How do I get through this without it getting worse?:A1_Q_CHOICE_MIXED
answer=I wanted someone to step in and take charge:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXTERNAL
answer=Like forces outside me were driving the day:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXTERNAL
answer=Like I had tried everything but nothing worked:A1_Q_CHOICE_MIXED
answer=Like I'd lost the thread and couldn't find it:A1_Q_CHOICE_MIXED
answer=Like I needed someone to rescue the situation:A1_Q_CHOICE_EXTERNAL

nodes.text: Think of one specific moment today when you had a real choice — even a small one. Which came closest to what you did?
options: I said something I didn't have to say, because it was right
I paused before reacting, and it changed how things went
I took on something uncomfortable rather than avoiding it
I let go of something I couldn't change and focused elsewhere

nodes.text: Even in a complicated day, there's usually one moment where you made a call. What did you do when things slipped?
options: I pushed through and hoped for the best
I asked for help or information
I stayed quiet when maybe I should have spoken
I adjusted my expectation of what was possible

nodes.text: On a hard day, sometimes we feel like we're just reacting. But somewhere in there — what was one thing only you could decide?
options: Whether I stayed engaged or mentally checked out
Whether I blamed others or stayed curious
Whether I kept trying or gave up early
Honestly, I'm not sure I had anything to decide today
signal_dominant=axis1:internal:A1_REFLECTION_INTERNAL
signal_dominant=axis1:external:A1_REFLECTION_EXTERNAL
signal_dominant=axis1:mixed:A1_REFLECTION_MIXED

nodes.text: What stands out is that you were in the driver's seat today — even if the road was bumpy. Noticing your own choices isn't self-congratulation. It's the practice of agency. It means tomorrow you'll see even more of them.

Tough days have a way of making the world feel like it's happening *to* us. That's honest. But somewhere in your answers is a small decision you made — maybe barely visible. That small decision is the door. You don't have to force it. Just notice it was there.
Most days are a mix — moments when you drove it, moments when the day drove you. That's realistic, not weak. What matters is the ratio is yours to shift, gradually, one small choice at a time.
You've looked at *how* you showed up today. Now let's look at *what you put into* the day — the giving side of your work.
When you think about your interactions today — with colleagues, a manager, a report, anyone — which frame best describes your dominant mindset?
options: I was focused on doing good work and helping things move
I was tracking whether my contribution was being noticed
I was aware of what the team needed, not just my own task
I felt frustrated that others weren't matching my effort
answer=I was focused on doing good work and helping things move:A2_Q_CONTRIBUTION
answer=I was tracking whether my contribution was being noticed:A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT
answer=I was aware of what the team needed, not just my own task:A2_Q_CONTRIBUTION
answer=I felt frustrated that others weren't matching my effort:A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT

nodes.text: You had a contribution orientation today. Was there a moment when you gave something that wasn't required of you?
options: Yes — I helped someone who was stuck, without being asked
Yes — I volunteered for something nobody wanted to own
Yes — I shared credit or knowledge that I could have kept
Not really — I stayed within my lane today

nodes.text: You mentioned tracking recognition or comparing effort. That's worth sitting with. What was the thought underneath it?
options: I genuinely feel undervalued here and it's affecting my motivation
I worked hard and wanted to know it mattered to someone
I don't know — it's become a habit to monitor fairness
I was having a hard day and it made me more self-focused
answer=Yes — I helped someone who was stuck, without being asked:A2_Q_DISCRETIONARY
answer=Yes — I volunteered for something nobody wanted to own:A2_Q_DISCRETIONARY
answer=Yes — I shared credit or knowledge that I could have kept:A2_Q_DISCRETIONARY
answer=Not really — I stayed within my lane today:A2_Q_AWARENESS
answer=I genuinely feel undervalued here and it's affecting my motivation:A2_Q_AWARENESS
answer=I worked hard and wanted to know it mattered to someone:A2_Q_AWARENESS
answer=I don't know — it's become a habit to monitor fairness:A2_Q_AWARENESS
answer=I was having a hard day and it made me more self-focused:A2_Q_DISCRETIONARY

nodes.text: Think of a colleague who had a harder day than you. Did anything make you aware of their struggle?
options: Yes, I noticed and said or did something about it
Yes, I noticed but didn't act on it
I was too absorbed in my own work to notice
I don't think anyone around me had a harder day

nodes.text: Contribution doesn't always mean doing extra — sometimes it's doing your part fully. Did you finish the day feeling like you'd actually delivered something today?
options: Yes — I can point to something real I moved forward
Partially — I tried but results felt incomplete
No — the day didn't feel productive even though I worked
I'm not sure what 'delivering' looks like in my current role
signal_dominant=axis2:contribution:A2_REFLECTION_CONTRIBUTION
signal_dominant=axis2:entitlement:A2_REFLECTION_ENTITLEMENT
signal_dominant=axis2:mixed:A2_REFLECTION_MIXED

nodes.text: You were giving today — not keeping score, just doing the work. That kind of orientation is invisible when it's happening, but it compounds. People remember who helped them when it wasn't required. So do you, quietly.

Noticing fairness, wanting recognition — there's nothing wrong with those impulses. But when they dominate, they narrow the field of view. Tomorrow, try one small act of invisible contribution — something only you'll know about. See what that does to the day.

Today had both — moments of giving and moments of wanting. That's common. The awareness itself is useful: when you notice you've shifted into entitlement mode, you can consciously move the other way. You've already got the skill — it's the noticing.
We've looked at how you responded to the day, and what you gave to it. Last part: let's widen the lens — who else was in your day?
When you think about today's biggest moment — a win, a difficulty, a decision — whose experience did you naturally think about?
options: Mine — it was my situation to navigate
My team's — we were in it together
A specific person who was affected more than I was
The people we're ultimately doing this work for
answer=Mine — it was my situation to navigate:A3_Q_SELF
answer=My team's — we were in it together:A3_Q_TEAM
answer=A specific person who was affected more than I was:A3_Q_OTHER
answer=The people we're ultimately doing this work for:A3_Q_OTHER

nodes.text: When you're in the middle of something hard, it makes sense to focus on yourself — that's self-preservation. But looking back now: was there someone else today whose experience you might have missed?
options: Yes — now that I think about it, someone was struggling nearby
Possibly — I was too in my head to notice
I don't think so — my situation was genuinely absorbing
I noticed others but it felt like I couldn't afford to care right then

nodes.text: You held the team in view today. Was there a moment when someone on the team needed something, and you felt it before they said it?
options: Yes — I noticed and acted on it
Yes — I noticed but wasn't sure how to respond
Not really — I stayed aware of them but didn't go beyond that
Actually no, looking back it was more about group outcomes than individuals

nodes.text: You thought about someone else — a colleague, a customer, a person this work affects. What happened in that moment of seeing them?
options: I did something differently because of it
It gave me a reason to keep going when I was tired
It made me feel connected to something bigger than my task
I noticed it but didn't know what to do with it
answer=Yes — now that I think about it, someone was struggling nearby:A3_Q_TRANSCEND
answer=Possibly — I was too in my head to notice:A3_Q_MEANING
answer=I don't think so — my situation was genuinely absorbing:A3_Q_MEANING
answer=I noticed others but it felt like I couldn't afford to care right then:A3_Q_TRANSCEND
answer=Yes — I noticed and acted on it:A3_Q_TRANSCEND
answer=Yes — I noticed but wasn't sure how to respond:A3_Q_MEANING
answer=Not really — I stayed aware of them but didn't go beyond that:A3_Q_MEANING
answer=Actually no, looking back it was more about group outcomes than individuals:A3_Q_MEANING
answer=I did something differently because of it:A3_Q_TRANSCEND
answer=It gave me a reason to keep going when I was tired:A3_Q_TRANSCEND
answer=It made me feel connected to something bigger than my task:A3_Q_TRANSCEND
answer=I noticed it but didn't know what to do with it:A3_Q_MEANING

nodes.text: When you thought about someone else today — their struggle, their need, their experience — what did that do to your own stress?
options: It reduced it — my problems felt smaller in proportion
It increased it — I took on their worry as my own
It didn't change it — the emotions were parallel, not competing
It gave me energy — caring for something outside me fuels me

nodes.text: At the end of a day where the lens was mostly on yourself — not wrong, just a pattern — what do you think made today feel meaningful or not?
options: Getting something done that I'd been stuck on
A conversation that felt real, not transactional
Helping someone, even briefly
Honestly, it didn't feel particularly meaningful today
signal_dominant=axis3:other:A3_REFLECTION_ALTRO
signal_dominant=axis3:team:A3_REFLECTION_TEAM
signal_dominant=axis3:self:A3_REFLECTION_SELF

nodes.text: Your radius today extended beyond yourself — to colleagues, to the people this work serves. Maslow called this self-transcendence: the paradox that we're most fully ourselves when we're oriented outward. You were there today.

You were team-aware today — thinking in 'we' rather than just 'I'. That collective orientation is rare and valuable. The next step, when it comes naturally, is expanding it still further: to the people the team is working *for*.

Today's frame was tight — focused on your work, your experience, your outcomes. That's not a flaw; sometimes survival mode is appropriate. But meaning tends to live just outside our own perimeter. Tomorrow, try noticing one person's experience alongside your own.

Today's reflection: You described today as "{OPEN_Q1.answer}". On agency, you leaned {axis1.dominant}. On contribution, you leaned {axis2.dominant}. On radius of concern, you leaned {axis3.dominant}.

These aren't verdicts — they're coordinates. You know where you were today. That's the whole point.

One question to carry into tomorrow: "{tomorrow_question}"
See you tomorrow. Rest well.

axis1.internal: internal locus — you saw your own hand in the day
axis1.external: external locus — the day felt like it happened to you
axis1.mixed: a mix of agency and circumstance

axis2.contribution: contribution — you were giving, not keeping score
axis2.mixed: a mix of giving and expecting

axis3.other: altrocentric — others were in your view
axis3.self: self-centric — the lens was mostly on you

tomorrow_questions.internal_contribution_other: Where today did someone need something you were uniquely positioned to give?
tomorrow_questions.internal_contribution_self: Tomorrow, before you start your own work — who on your team is struggling?
tomorrow_questions.internal_entitlement_other: Was there a moment today when the recognition you wanted would have meant more to someone else?
tomorrow_questions.internal_entitlement_self: What would you do differently if you knew no one was watching?
tomorrow_questions.external_contribution_other: When you helped someone today, was that a choice — or just reaction?
tomorrow_questions.external_contribution_self: What's one thing tomorrow where you don't wait for permission to act?
tomorrow_questions.external_entitlement_other: You noticed unfairness today. Did anyone else notice yours?
tomorrow_questions.external_entitlement_self: Tomorrow, what's one small thing you'll do that only you will know about?
tomorrow_questions.mixed_mixed_mixed: What would it look like to take 10% more ownership of tomorrow than you did today?
